page: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Eadsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, positioning the server within the memory-optimized category.

For local storage, the E2ads_v7 includes a 118 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. This local disk capacity provides a direct storage resource for temporary files or caching. The instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, meaning external networking must be configured separately. No graphics processing units are present on this server type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2ads_v7 offers a high memory density relative to its low vCPU count, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial memory capacity without demanding high parallel processing power. The primary tradeoff is the limited compute capacity of only 2 vCPUs, which may bottleneck CPU-bound applications. This hardware profile is suitable for small-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, and lightweight enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine within the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with a 118 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, providing fast local storage options for temporary files or caching. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this server cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require extensive parallel processing power. Tradeoffs include the low compute capacity of 2 vCPUs, which limits its suitability for CPU-heavy tasks. This configuration is designed for workloads such as small-scale databases, in-memory caches, and lightweight enterprise applications.
